articles.title: Tax breaks reward those who can afford to take risks. What about the rest of us?

items.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians

children.content: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.

items.title: Will the war in Iran make Australia use less plastic?

children.content: Could the war in Iran change the way we recycle back home?
Australia mostly relies on importing plastic from overseas.
As well as petrol and diesel, the Strait of Hormuz is a key delivery route for a crucial backbone of plastic production. But, that's now cut off.
It's led to a growing conversation about how Australia might transition to fully recycling plastic packaging onshore.

image.altText: TN_PlasticIran

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for
